text: This is the VOA Special English Health Report.
zh: 这里是美国之音特别英语健康报告。

text: How many calories we eat appears to be more important than what we eat when we eat too much.
zh: 当我们吃得过多时，摄入多少卡路里似乎比吃什么更重要。

text: That was the finding of a new study that should be satisfying to anyone who counts calories for weight control.
zh: 这是一项新研究的发现，对任何靠计算卡路里来控制体重的人来说，这个结果都令人满意。

text: More than one billion adults are overweight.
zh: 全球超过十亿成年人超重。

text: weight. The World Health Organization calls obesity a global epidemic.
zh: 世界卫生组织称肥胖是一种全球流行病。

text: Government researchers have just reported their latest estimates for the United States.
zh: 美国政府研究人员刚刚公布了最新的估算数据。

text: The good news, obesity rates have not increased much in recent years.
zh: 好消息是，近年来肥胖率没有大幅上升。

text: The bad news, they have not decreased either.
zh: 坏消息是，肥胖率也没有下降。

text: More than one -third of adults were obese in 2009 and 2010.
zh: 2009年至2010年间，超过三分之一的成年人属于肥胖。

text: Anyone who has ever tried to lose weight knows there are all kinds of conflicting diet plans.
zh: 任何尝试过减肥的人都知道，市面上有各种相互矛盾的饮食方案。

text: Some tell people not to eat carbohydrates. Others say not to eat fats.
zh: 有的建议不吃碳水化合物，有的则说不要吃脂肪。

text: Still others say to eat more protein or to eat less protein.
zh: 还有人说要多摄入蛋白质，或者要少摄入蛋白质。

text: Dr. George Bray at the Pennington Biomedical Research Center in Louisiana led the new study.
zh: 路易斯安那州彭宁顿生物医学研究中心的乔治·布雷博士主持了这项新研究。

text: He wanted to know if protein affects weight gain.
zh: 他想知道蛋白质是否会影响体重增加。

text: 25 healthy adults were in the study.
zh: 研究对象是25名健康成年人。

text: They were overfed by almost 1 ,000 calories a day over an eight -week period.
zh: 在为期八周的时间里，他们每天多摄入近1000卡路里。

text: Calories in food are a measure of energy.
zh: 食物中的卡路里是能量的度量单位。

text: The people followed diets with low, normal, or high levels of protein.
zh: 参与者分别采用低、正常或高蛋白质水平的饮食。

text: Normal was defined as 15 % of energy from protein.
zh: 正常水平被定义为蛋白质提供15%的能量。

text: Dr. Bray says all of them gained weight.
zh: 布雷博士说，所有人的体重都增加了。

text: Fat storage was exactly the same with all three levels of protein.
zh: 无论蛋白质水平高低，脂肪储存量都完全相同。

text: It was the calories that they ate that affected the body fat that they stored.
zh: 真正影响体内脂肪储存的是摄入的卡路里总量。

text: Those who ate a higher percentage of protein gained more lean body mass.
zh: 摄入蛋白质比例较高的人增加了更多的瘦体重。

text: Dr. Bray says the opposite happened on a low protein diet.
zh: 布雷博士说，低蛋白饮食则出现相反的情况。

text: If your protein intake's low, you'll actually lose body mass
zh: 如果蛋白质摄入量低，你实际上会流失体重，

text: even though you're eating an excess amount of calories.
zh: 即使你摄入了过量的卡路里。

text: He tells his patients to weigh themselves regularly so they can know quickly if they gain weight.
zh: 他告诉自己的病人要经常称体重，这样就能及时发现体重增加。

text: His study appeared in the Journal of the American Medical Association.
zh: 他的研究发表在《美国医学会杂志》上。

text: Dr. Dariush Mozaffarian at the Harvard School of Public Health
zh: 哈佛大学公共卫生学院的达里乌什·莫扎法里安博士

text: studies the relationship between diet and conditions like heart disease and diabetes.
zh: 研究饮食与心脏病、糖尿病等疾病之间的关系。

text: He says we don't need to go down a list of avoid this, avoid that,
zh: 他说，我们不需要列出一长串“不要吃这个、不要吃那个”的清单，

text: become, you know, food police. It's really actually mostly foods that should be
zh: 变成所谓的“食物警察”。实际上，大多数情况下应该是

text: increased. It's fruits, vegetables, whole grains, fish, vegetable oils, and nuts.
zh: 增加某些食物的摄入，比如水果、蔬菜、全谷物、鱼类、植物油和坚果。

text: He says increasing these six foods by about one serving each day
zh: 他说，如果每天将这六类食物的摄入量各增加一份，

text: would reduce obesity and help slow the epidemic of obesity -related diseases.
zh: 就能减少肥胖，并有助于减缓与肥胖相关疾病的流行。

text: For VOA Special English, I'm Alex Villarreal.
zh: 这里是美国之音特别英语节目，我是亚历克斯·维拉里尔。

text: You can find a video about the study at voaspecialenglish .com.
zh: 您可以在voaspecialenglish.com上找到关于这项研究的视频。
